chapters.title: Identify the Vāra
chapters.content_preview: 1. Identify the Vāra  Use the weekday as per the local panchang convention. In many traditional panchangs, Vāra is sunrise-to-sunrise rather than midnight-to-midnight, so local sunrise matters for edge cases.  2. Identify the Moon’s Nakshatra  The yoga changes when the Moon chan

chapters.title: Identify the Moon’s Nakshatra
chapters.content_preview: 2. Identify the Moon’s Nakshatra  The yoga changes when the Moon changes nakshatra. If the nakshatra changes mid-day, the Vāra–Nakṣatra yoga changes at that transition.  3. Match the row  Find the weekday row and compare the current nakshatra against the four cells: Utpāta, Mṛty

chapters.title: Match the row
chapters.content_preview: 3. Match the row  Find the weekday row and compare the current nakshatra against the four cells: Utpāta, Mṛtyu, Kāṇa, or Siddhi.      4. Interpret conservatively  Siddhi is supportive, but it is not a blank cheque. For serious muhurta selection, still examine tithi, tara bala, c

chapters.title: Interpret conservatively
chapters.content_preview: 4. Interpret conservatively  Siddhi is supportive, but it is not a blank cheque. For serious muhurta selection, still examine tithi, tara bala, chandra bala, lagna, yoga, karana, weekday defects, rāhu kāla/yamaganda, and the purpose of the activity.  Working rule: Avoid Utpāta,
